body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Scope

This cell is the FULL user-blocking-question arc: the agent ends a turn with a clarifying question (working‚Üíwaiting), the USER then answers, and the agent finishes the turn (waiting‚Üíworking‚Üíready), all on the same live rollout session. It extends the sibling `agent-question-pending` cell (same coverage_id `user-blocking-question`), which proved only the ENTRY into waiting (working‚Üíwaiting) and deliberately stopped there. The scenario-meanings.md user-observable signal is explicit: `ready ‚Üí working ‚Üí waiting`; after the user replies, `waiting ‚Üí working ‚Üí ready`; the waiting episode is visible (no skip-over).

Note: codex has NO structured AskUserQuestion tool (matrix prior). This cell, like its sibling, exercises the adapter-agnostic trailing-`?` text path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput ‚Üí state_classifier.go decision 2a), not a dedicated question tool. The scenario.json verify block names this path directly.

## agent_supports: yes

codex can be prompted to end its turn with a single clarifying question (literal `?`). It records the reply as a `response_item`/`message`(assistant) whose `output_text` carries the question, followed by an `event_msg`/`task_complete` turn-finish marker ‚Äî confirmed against the sibling's recorded rollout. A subsequent user answer is just another turn on the same rollout session: codex resumes working and completes normally, ending the final turn in a non-question terminal reply (‚Üí ready). This is standard multi-turn behaviour already proven by the multi-turn-conversation recording.

## irrlicht_observes: yes (interactive TUI, not headless)

The codex adapter is `FilesUnderRoot{Dir: ~/.codex/sessions, JSONLineParser}`. The parser maps the assistant message to `assistant_message` (sets `AssistantText`) and `event_msg`/`task_complete` to `turn_done`. At the first turn's `task_complete`, `IsAgentDone()` is true AND `LastAssistantText` ends in `?`, so the classifier takes decision 2a (waiting) rather than 2b (ready). When the user sends the answer, the session goes working again; the final turn ends in a plain reply with no trailing `?`, so the classifier takes 2b (ready). The waiting‚Üíworking‚Üíready recovery is therefore observable on the SAME session_id.

The load-bearing constraint (inherited from the sibling) is session liveness: headless `codex exec` is single-shot and exits at turn end (process_exited race, see basic-turn spec), so the multi-turn arc would be lost. The interactive driver (drive-codex-interactive.sh, `codex --no-alt-screen` under tmux) keeps the rollout session ALIVE across both turns; the REPL is torn down only at script end. A trailing sleep after each wait_turn lets each state episode settle.

## Recipe implication

INTERACTIVE `script`: send a prompt that makes codex ask one clarifying question ‚Üí wait_turn ‚Üí sleep (settle into waiting) ‚Üí send the ANSWER ‚Üí wait_turn ‚Üí sleep (settle into ready). The first reply must end in `?`; the final reply must NOT, so the terminal state is ready.
caveats: Observable ONLY via the interactive TUI ‚Äî headless `codex exec` exits at turn end and both the waiting episode and the recovery would be lost to a process_exited race (basic-turn spec documents this). The recipe must be interactive.
Distinct from the structured AskUserQuestion path: codex has no question tool in its schema (matrix prior), so this is the trailing-`?` text classifier (decision 2a), which is adapter-agnostic.

The first reply must end in a literal `?` (‚Üí waiting) and the FINAL reply after the user's answer must NOT end in `?` (‚Üí ready). Steer the answer prompt so codex confirms/acts and ends on a statement, otherwise the terminal turn would re-enter waiting instead of ready.
Extends the sibling agent-question-pending (same coverage_id) which locked down only working‚Üíwaiting; this cell adds the waiting‚Üíworking‚Üíready recovery after the user answers.

sources.note: sibling cell, same coverage_id user-blocking-question: yes/yes for the trailing-? entry into waiting; this cell extends it with the post-answer recovery
user-blocking-question user-observable signal: ready‚Üíworking‚Üíwaiting, then after user reply waiting‚Üíworking‚Üíready; waiting episode visible (no skip-over)
IsWaitingForUserInput via ExtractQuestionSnippet (trailing ?); a non-question terminal reply drops to 2b (ready)
assistant_message sets AssistantText; event_msg/task_complete ‚Üí turn_done; FilesUnderRoot watch
proves the interactive codex driver produces real per-turn working‚Üîready arcs on a single live rollout session ‚Äî the basis for the waiting‚Üíworking‚Üíready recovery here
interactive driver keeps the REPL alive across turns (kill-session only at script end); supports send/wait_turn/sleep ‚Äî the steps this recipe needs
